body.title: [sprint] Google Secretary: login + 2FA + credential management
body.description:
Extend GoogleSecretaryDevice with the ability to perform browser-based login flows including 2FA, and to create/manage API keys for Google services. Gated on T-google-secretary-device so the core device ships first.

Design: SWADL page/flow objects handle the login sequence (username → password → 2FA challenge → success). Secretary stores credentials in an encrypted flat-file or Postgres secrets table (never in source). 2FA handling: TOTP from a secrets store, or escalate-to-human for push/SMS 2FA. API key creation flow uses the same SWADL page-object pattern.

**Affected files:** devices/google_secretary/auth/ (new — login_flow.py, totp.py, credential_store.py); devices/google_secretary/device.py (add login capability); tests/google_secretary/test_auth.py (new)

**Design rules:** no-sqlite; no live keys in source (.env gitignored); bus+shim model; log every auth event at INFO

**Scope boundary:** IN — SWADL-based login flow; TOTP 2FA from secrets store; escalate-to-human for push/SMS 2FA; API key creation via SWADL; credential store (encrypted flat-file or Postgres). OUT — storing raw passwords anywhere; SMS intercept; credential rotation automation (follow-on)

**Completion criteria:** (1) login_flow.py can replay a mocked Google login sequence via SWADL; (2) TOTP generation works given a test seed; (3) ambiguous 2FA (push/SMS) triggers escalate-to-channel; (4) credential_store writes/reads without touching source or .git; (5) all tests/google_secretary/test_auth.py green; (6) no credentials appear in git diff
